post_topic: HITL gates that can actually say no — the governance reality behind multi-agent swarms
image_description:
Single standalone diagram. One concept only. No panels, no grids, no collages. Full bleed 1080x1080px white background.

A left-to-right flow diagram titled 'HITL Gate: Rejection Is a First-Class Path' in bold navy at the top.

On the far left, a box labeled 'Orchestrator Agent' in navy. An arrow points right to a box labeled 'Proposal Generated' in blue.

From 'Proposal Generated', an arrow points right to a central diamond decision node labeled 'Human Review Gate' in bold amber with a person icon inside.

From the diamond, two arrows branch: one pointing right labeled 'Approve' in blue, leading to a box labeled 'Execution' in navy. One pointing downward labeled 'Reject' in amber, leading to a box labeled 'Rejection Handler' in amber.

From 'Rejection Handler', three smaller arrows fan out downward-right to three boxes in a vertical column: first box labeled 'Capture Reason' in blue, second box labeled 'Feed Back as Constraint' in blue, third box labeled 'Audit Log' in blue.

A curved arrow from 'Rejection Handler' loops back left toward 'Orchestrator Agent', labeled 'Revised Proposal' in amber, completing the governance loop.

Below the entire diagram, a small footer note in gray reads: 'If rejection has no defined path, it is not governance.'

Navy #0A0F1E primary elements, blue #5a96ff highlights, amber #F5A623 callouts and important labels. Clean sans-serif font. Real cloud provider logos where relevant. Small text 'Kelvin Davis' bottom right corner. Professional LinkedIn infographic style similar to ByteByteGo. White background. No dark backgrounds.
